most_recent_enforcement_milestone.summary: Governance and obligations for providers of general-purpose AI (GPAI) models applied on 2 August 2025; initial 'first rules' (definitions, AI literacy, certain prohibitions) applied on 2 February 2025.

primary_sources.title: Navigating the AI Act (European Commission)
primary_sources.note: Commission FAQ lists application dates 2 Feb 2025, 2 Aug 2025, 2 Aug 2026; confirms GPAI obligations applicability 2 Aug 2025.

primary_sources.title: Commission publishes guidelines for providers of general-purpose AI models
primary_sources.note: Press release confirming GPAI guidance and 2 Aug 2025 applicability for GPAI obligations.

next_obligations.summary: 2 August 2026 is the next major compliance date when most high-risk obligations (Annex III) and Article 50 transparency requirements become applicable, including risk-management, data governance, technical documentation, human oversight, conformity assessments, EU database registration, and post-market monitoring.

primary_sources.title: Artificial Intelligence Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) - Official Journal
primary_sources.note: Legal text with Article 113 staggered application dates and Article 72 post-market monitoring requirement.

items: Status of Commission implementing acts/templates (notably Article 72 post-market monitoring plan template) — deadline 2 Feb 2026 but unclear whether template was published; check OJ/EUR-Lex and AI Office pages.
Availability of harmonised standards / common specifications and notified bodies — capacity bottlenecks could delay practical conformity assessments.
Possible legislative adjustments (Digital Omnibus proposal) that could alter application dates or link dates to availability of supporting measures.
Classification disputes (whether a system is 'high-risk' or qualifies as GPAI) — legal interpretation may vary and may require AI Office/national authority decisions or case law.
Sectoral interactions with other EU product safety and sector laws (medical devices, vehicles, toys), leading to different clocks/requirements.
key_uncertainties.advice: Treat 2 Aug 2026 as the scheduled next major date but prepare now: classify systems, document risk-management, build post-market monitoring capability, and monitor Commission/OJ/AI Office updates weekly.

source_notes.title: European Commission - Navigating the AI Act (FAQ)
